notes: Christian priest mentioned by Gregory the Great; also referenced as a skilled soldier by Ammianus Marcellinus.

docs.title: BOOK XIV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: He, alarmed at his own danger when he saw the corrupt accusers and judges who were associated with him, all emerging out of the same lurking-places, wrote secret letters to Constantius informing him of what was going on,

docs.title: BOOK XV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: 3. But this magnanimous man stood his ground immovably against whatever might happen, only taking care not to throw himself away in an abject manner, and grieving from his heart that innocence had no safe foundation on which to stand.

docs.title: BOOK XVIII
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: 3. And by this base compliance many tried to purchase the favour of Eusebius, at that time the principal chamberlain, with whom (if we are to say the real truth) Constantius had great influence, and who was now a bitter
